task_completion.description: Verify that at least one official OSHA Fact Sheet or Quick Card (PDF) was downloaded to ~/Downloads.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing ~/Downloads containing a PDF file related to OSHA, or Firefox download history/popup showing the completed download of an OSHA PDF.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a Firefox bookmark folder named 'OSHA Construction Safety'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox bookmark toolbar, menu, or Library (bookmark manager) showing a folder exactly named 'OSHA Construction Safety'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'OSHA Construction Safety' folder contains at least four bookmarks, one for each of the Fatal Four hazard categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'OSHA Construction Safety' bookmark folder displayed in Firefox, showing at least four saved web pages corresponding to Falls, Struck-By, Electrocution, and Caught-In/Between hazards.

task_completion.description: Verify that a summary file named 'safety_compliance_checklist.txt' was created in ~/Documents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing ~/Documents/safety_compliance_checklist.txt, or a text editor/terminal open with this file path.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary file contains section headers for each of the Fatal Four hazards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of safety_compliance_checklist.txt showing distinct sections or headers for Falls, Struck-By Objects, Electrocution, and Caught-In/Between.

task_completion.description: Verify that the summary file includes specific 29 CFR 1926 standard numbers and at least one key compliance requirement for each hazard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text of safety_compliance_checklist.txt showing OSHA standard numbers (e.g., 1926.501, 1926 Subpart K) and specific compliance rules (e.g., fall protection at 6 feet, lockout/tagout procedures) under each hazard section.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Firefox browser to research and download the PDF.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing Firefox open and navigating OSHA websites, rather than terminal commands like curl or wget being used to fetch the PDF.

integrity.description: Ensure bookmarks were created via the browser GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing interaction with the Firefox bookmarking interface (star icon, bookmark manager), rather than terminal scripts modifying Firefox profile databases.

integrity.description: Ensure the summary file contains actual researched information and not just copied prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The compliance requirements in the text file should be specific safety rules (e.g., guardrail heights, excavation cave-in protection) that require actual research, not just generic placeholders or repeated prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Fatal Four' hazards are Falls, Struck-By, Electrocution, and Caught-In/Between. OSHA construction standards are found in 29 CFR 1926. Valid standard numbers include: Falls (1926.501 or Subpart M), Electrocution (1926 Subpart K, e.g., 1926.403), Caught-In/Between (1926 Subpart P, e.g., 1926.651 for excavations), and Struck-By (multiple standards including 1926 Subpart H, 1926.250, 1926.602). OSHA publishes free Quick Cards and Fact Sheets as downloadable PDFs.
